title: Loop Menu Configuration
description: Defines the sidebar Loop tab menu structure — groups, buttons, and prompt file references.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IDE validation.

$comment.description: Human-readable description of this config file.

version.description: Schema version for future migration support.

projectType.description: Project type hint used for template selection.

groups.description: Ordered list of button groups shown in the Loop tab.

projectPhase.description: Current project lifecycle phase. Controls which groups are expanded and which buttons are prominent.

group.description: A collapsible section of related action buttons.

id.description: Unique identifier for the group. Used for state persistence.

label.description: Display label for the group header.

desc.description: Short description shown below the group header.

icon.description: Codicon name for the group header icon (without 'codicon-' prefix).

accent.description: CSS color for the group accent bar.

collapsed.description: Whether the group is collapsed by default.

phase.description: Project phases where this group is active. Omit to show in all phases.

source.description: Origin of this group: 'core' (default config), 'type' (project type template), or 'skill' (skill partial).

buttons.description: Ordered list of action buttons in this group.

button.description: A single action button that triggers a command or opens a chat prompt.

id.description: Unique identifier for frecency tracking. Defaults to lowercase label if omitted.

promptFile.description: Filename of the .prompt.md file in .github/prompts/loop/. Loaded at click time.

prompt.description: Inline prompt text. Used as fallback when promptFile is not set.

file.description: URL or file path for openExternal/runCommand actions.

hint.description: Visual hint icon for button behavior.

phase.description: Project phases where this button is visible. Omit to show in all phases.

releaseScript.description: Path to the release automation script.

conventions.description: List of project conventions and technologies.
